seoTitle: How to Add Backlog View to a Jira Kanban Board
metaDescription: Enable the backlog view on a Jira Kanban board so you can plan and prioritize upcoming work separately from the active board.
intro: Kanban boards don't show a backlog view by default the way Scrum boards do, which can make forward planning harder. This guide covers turning on the backlog view for a Kanban board in Jira.

steps.heading: Open your Kanban board settings
steps.body: Go to the board and find its settings menu where general board features are configured.

steps.heading: Enable the backlog feature
steps.body: Turn on the backlog option so a separate view is created for unscheduled or unstarted work.

steps.heading: Move issues into the backlog
steps.body: Send lower-priority or unplanned issues to the backlog view so the main board stays focused on active work.

steps.heading: Prioritize the backlog list
steps.body: Reorder backlog issues so the most important upcoming work sits at the top.

steps.heading: Pull issues onto the board when ready
steps.body: Move issues from the backlog onto the active Kanban board as capacity opens up.

faq.question: Is a backlog view available on every Kanban board?
faq.answer: It needs to be enabled specifically, since Kanban boards don't show a backlog view by default.

faq.question: How is the Kanban backlog different from a Scrum backlog?
faq.answer: The Kanban backlog holds unscheduled work without sprint boundaries, while a Scrum backlog is organized around sprints.

faq.question: Can I set work-in-progress limits from the backlog view?
faq.answer: WIP limits are typically set on the active board's columns rather than in the backlog view itself.

faq.question: Do backlog issues show up in reports?
faq.answer: Some reports include backlog issues if your filter captures them, though most reporting focuses on active board columns.
